content: Brain Twin summary card presenting baseline neurophysiological profile with moderate confidence. For clinical decision support only. Not a substitute for professional judgment. Key findings include elevated frontal theta power and reduced alpha coherence patterns consistent with attention-related profiles. Data provenance includes EEG recording session and automated analysis pipeline.

sections.body: Subject identifier hash: a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0c1d2e3f4a5b6c7d8e9f0a1b2. This is a de-identified SHA-256 hash of the subject identifier. No personally identifiable information is contained in this summary card. The hash allows longitudinal tracking without exposing patient identity. Hash generation follows NIST SP 800-107 recommendations for cryptographic hashing of sensitive identifiers.

Baseline neurophysiological metrics derived from quantitative EEG assessment: Peak alpha frequency 9.8 Hz (within normal range). Frontal theta/beta ratio 3.2 (elevated, 1.5 SD above normative mean). Global alpha power 12.3 uV2 (within normal limits). Interhemispheric frontal coherence 0.45 (reduced, 1.3 SD below normative mean). Default mode network connectivity index 0.62 (mildly reduced). Attention network efficiency metric 0.71 (below expected threshold of 0.80). These metrics establish the pre-intervention baseline for longitudinal monitoring.

Key neurophysiological findings from integrated assessment: (1) Elevated frontal theta activity suggesting reduced cortical activation in prefrontal regions. (2) Reduced interhemispheric alpha coherence indicating altered functional connectivity between hemispheres. (3) Attention network metrics below expected thresholds suggesting functional impairment in sustained attention circuitry. (4) Preserved posterior alpha rhythm suggesting intact thalamocortical function. (5) Normal beta and gamma band activity indicating no evidence of cortical irritability. These findings form a coherent neurophysiological profile consistent with attention-related functional patterns.

Overall confidence tier: moderate. This classification is based on the following factors: (1) Data quality is high with adequate signal-to-noise ratio and sufficient artifact-free recording duration. (2) Three citations with valid PMIDs support the interpretive framework. (3) Automated analysis pipeline passed all quality control checks. (4) Normative database comparison uses age and sex-matched reference data. Factors limiting confidence: single recording session, no longitudinal data yet available, and concurrent medication may influence some metrics. Confidence may be upgraded to high with replication of findings in a follow-up assessment.
For clinical decision support only. Not a substitute for professional judgment.

Data sources: (1) 19-channel quantitative EEG recording performed on 2024-01-15 using NeuroPrax system with sampling rate 256 Hz. (2) Automated spectral and connectivity analysis via DeepSynaps qEEG pipeline version 2.1.0. (3) Normative database comparison using age/sex-matched reference data (n=1200 healthy controls). (4) Quality control validation performed by automated QC pipeline with manual review confirmation. Processing pipeline version: deepsynaps-qeeg-pipeline v2.1.0. Analysis timestamp: 2024-01-15T14:30:00Z. All raw data retained in encrypted storage with 7-year retention policy per institutional requirements.

citations.title: EEG coherence in ADHD: a meta-analysis
Frontal theta activity and cognitive functioning
